name: Hydro Boost Water Gel
hero: A hyaluronic-led gel that wears beautifully on oily and combination skin in summer. Modest claims hold; the marketing claims ("deeply quenches") don't. Use it for what it is — a light hydrator, not a barrier-repair cream.

scoreBreakdown.n: Best-in-class water-gel feel for humid climates.
HA blend is real but the rest is fragrance and filler.
₹ 850 / 50 mL — fair for the texture, weak for the formula.

ingredients.note: Negative: fragrance is the most common cosmetic allergen.

useCases.k: Oily-skin daily AM moisturiser in summer
useCases.b: Pleasant under SPF; doesn't pile or pill.
As a humectant base before an occlusive.
Light, absorbs fast, won't stain a t-shirt collar.

alts.name: Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5
alts.note: Cheaper humectant equivalent in serum form.

faq.q: Does the hyaluronic acid 'plump' my skin?
faq.a: Surface hydration improves the look of fine lines temporarily. There's no dermal volume change — that requires injection, not topical HA.
For dry-to-normal in summer, yes. For genuinely dry skin year-round, layer a heavier cream over it. Hydro Boost alone is humectant-only.
If you have a history of contact reactions, yes. Pick La Roche-Posay Hyalu B5 instead. For most users it's tolerated, but the fragrance is decorative, not functional.
Gel-Cream is heavier — has dimethicone-driven occlusion. Better in winter or for drier skin. Gel is the summer / oily default.

sources.n: Pavicic T et al. Hyaluronic acid in skin hydration. JDD 2011.
Bukhari SNA et al. Hyaluronic acid: a key biomolecule. Int J Biol Macromol 2018.
Neutrogena Hydro Boost dossier — clinical hydration data. 2021.
